$comment: Stripe product/price seed for paid plans + one-time Event Pass. Idempotent source of truth: `npm run stripe:sync` ensures each product + price exists in the Stripe account of STRIPE_SECRET_KEY (matched by price lookup_key), keeps multi-currency price points (currency_options) in sync on every run, and writes the resulting price ids into the `plans` table (stripe_price_id_monthly/annual, stripe_price_id_onetime for passes). Re-run after any DB wipe, and against each environment (test/prod) by pointing STRIPE_SECRET_KEY + DATABASE_URL at it. Non-USD amounts are SET price points (v3/07 §4), never FX conversions. Community is free (no Stripe).
$comment_tiers: Billing groups (spec 2026-07-21 §Pricing): a subscription covers several organisations, so each paid price is a GRADUATED tiered price whose quantity is the number of organisations in the group. Tier 1 is the plan base; tier 2+ is an extra organisation at half the base. `unit_amount`/`currency_options` stay in place and always equal tier 1 — they are what a group of one pays, and what the pricing page and lib/currency.ts advertise. Per-currency tier amounts are SET price points like every other amount here, derived as half the tier-1 point rounded DOWN to a whole major unit (INR down to the nearest x99, matching its existing charm points) — never an FX conversion.

product.name: Seazn Club Pro
product.description: Unlimited competitions, multi-division, online registration + entry fees, realtime scoreboard, custom branding, CSV/PDF exports. Covers up to 5 organisations on one bill — each extra organisation is half the base rate.

product.name: Seazn Club Pro Plus
product.description: Everything in Pro, plus unlimited members, scorers and clubs inside every organisation, a 1% platform fee on entry fees, AI-assisted scheduling, auto officials assignment, write API access and priority support. Covers up to 10 organisations on one bill — each extra organisation is half the base rate.

product.name: Seazn Club Event Pass
product.description: One-time upgrade for a single competition: 10 divisions, 32 entrants per division, advanced formats, entry fees, branding, exports and realtime — for the event's lifetime.
